mechanism_narrative: With FEDFUNDS held near the zero lower bound (0.97%), the persistent low level of the policy rate keeps the equity discount rate compressed, supporting a positive drift in forward S&P 500 returns as long as the rate stays below its trailing 12-month average. This is an always-on carry/discount-rate effect independent of any specific trigger.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=0.97 as of t-1mo, near historic lows
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 20d forward returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns during low-rate periods are not significantly higher than the full-sample mean (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE of more than 0.3 percentage points over a rolling 3-month window signals emerging labor-market deterioration that historically precedes risk-off repricing in equities, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days. The signal is inactive when UNRATE is flat or declining, as observed currently (6.5%, on a declining trend from prior cycle peak).
failure_condition: Among triggered instances (UNRATE rising >0.3pp in 3 months), 20-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in INDPRO (industrial output) and PAYEMS (nonfarm payrolls) growth below their respective trailing 6-month trend rates signals a joint supply-and-labor slowdown that is not captured by either series alone, and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter (60 trading days) as earnings growth expectations reprice downward.
candidate_trigger: both INDPRO and PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth fall below their trailing 6-month average simultaneously
failure_condition: Joint-trigger instances show 60-day forward returns positive in more than 55% of cases, or the interaction term's marginal Rank IC contribution over either single-series model is not statistically distinguishable from zero (bootstrap p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery expansion regime, elevated housing starts (HOUST) above 800k units signal strengthening consumer demand and construction activity that feed through to broader economic momentum and corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. This effect is expected to weaken or reverse once the expansion matures and rates rise significantly, since housing becomes rate-sensitive and a late-cycle drag.
regime_hint: post-recession recovery expansion with near-zero policy rates
failure_condition: During periods with HOUST>800k and FEDFUNDS<2%, 60-day forward SPX returns are negative in more than 50% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window in this regime.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in RSAFS (retail sales) reflects real-time consumer spending strength, which is a leading indicator of near-term corporate revenue surprises; sustained positive RSAFS growth is associated with positive S&P 500 returns over the following trading week (5 days), reflecting fast incorporation of consumption strength into equity prices. This is an always-on trend signal.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS MoM growth and 5-day forward SPX returns is below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive RSAFS growth months do not show statistically higher forward returns (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in INDPRO below its trailing 6-month moving average signals an emerging manufacturing/industrial contraction that has historically led broader economic slowdowns, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days as investors reprice cyclical earnings risk.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its trailing 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Triggered instances (INDPRO below 6-month MA) show positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, the joint behavior of rising FEDFUNDS alongside accelerating CPIAUCSL inflation compresses real rates and equity valuation multiples simultaneously, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter; the effect requires both series to move together, since rate hikes without inflation acceleration (or vice versa) do not reliably compress equity valuations to the same degree.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rising for 3+ consecutive months while CPIAUCSL YoY growth exceeds 2.5%
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with above-target inflation
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes show 60-day forward returns positive in more than 50% of instances, or the interaction model's out-of-sample Rank IC does not exceed the best single-series model by a statistically significant margin.

mechanism_narrative: Continued month-over-month growth in PAYEMS reflects ongoing labor market expansion that supports household income and consumption, translating into modest positive S&P 500 returns over the following trading week. This is an always-on trend effect that operates independent of any specific threshold.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS MoM change and 5-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive-payroll-growth months fail to show significantly higher forward returns (p>0.05).
